svg.text: how the canisters, the off-chain deploy service, and Casals wire together (test / demo / staging on IC mainnet)
Both paths build/store artifacts in file_registry and share the realm_installer domain steps (extensions, codices, registration).

g.text: user creates a realm in browser

svg.text: dfx install backend WASM + deploy frontend
Legacy fast path - slated for retirement by Casals (CASALS_MIGRATION.md)
realms register here to be listed
one Casals Stand per realm (agora / dominion / syntropia)
Both deploy paths land here: Casals upgrades on-chain, or the deploy
service installs via dfx; installer then adds extensions/codices.
Controllers: Casals + CycleOps only (no human key on orchestra canisters)
platform-shared services - upgraded via Casals only
(these same canisters appear above as live services; here they are also upgrade targets)
publish-build → file_registry + authorize_wasm; rollout → Casals.upgrade_to pulls
artifacts and upgrades realms + infra on-chain (snapshot/verify/rollback built in).
The deploy service is NOT involved on this path.
wizard / mundus → registry.request_deployment → installer queues job → deploy
service polls, installs WASM+frontend via dfx, reports back → installer finishes
extensions/codices + register_realm. Docs: AGENTS.md | CASALS_ROLLOUT.md | CASALS_MIGRATION.md
